raw.population: [PK, total] N: 1217 AGE: (2-85) [PK, paediatrics] N: 258 AGE: (2-15) [PKPD, adults] N: 1536 AGE: 18-85 [PKPD, paediatrics] N: 360 AGE: 2-17
raw.objective: “To identify and validate the efficacious monotherapy dosing regimen for topiramate in children aged 2 to <10 years with newly diagnosed epilepsy using pharmacokinetic–pharmacodynamic (PK–PD) modeling and simulation bridging.”
raw.covariates: PK Tested: age, body weight, serum creatinine, concomitant medications, and adjuvant treatment Included: weight, age, and AEDs on CL. Weight on the central volume of distribution PKPD (hazard) Tested: age, baseline number of seizures (3 moths prior to the study) Included: baseline number of seizures
raw.observations: A two-compartmental population PK model with first-order absorption described the time course of topiramate CMIN. Disposition of topiramate was related to age, body weight, and use of various concomitant antiepileptic drugs. The PK–PD model for monotherapy indicated that the hazard of time to first seizure decreased with increasing CMIN and time since randomization. Higher baseline seizure frequency increased risk for seizures. Based on the available PK–PD modeling data, the dosing regimen expected to achieve a 65–75% seizure freedom rate after 1 year for pediatric patients age 2–10 years is approximately 6–9 mg/kg per day.
raw.structure: PK: 2-compartment model with first order absorption and elimination PD: hazard model
raw.equations: [PK] FCWT=((WT/69.9)**theta3) FCAGE=exp(theta4*(AGE-31.4)) FCIN=theta5**INMD FCVP=theta6**VPA FCNE=theta7**NEMD CL=theta1*FCWT*FCAGE*FCIN*FCVP*FCNE Vd=theta8*FVWT*exp(eta2) K12=theta11 K21=theta12 Ka=theta10*exp(eta3) INMD is inducers given (carbamazepine, phenobarbital or phenytoin; 0=no; 1=yes) VPA is comedication with valproic acid (0=no; 1=yes) NEMD is comedication with any other type not known to be an inducer or inhibitor of CYP450 enzymes (0=no; 1=yes) [PD] E0=theta1 ESLOPE=theta2 ECMIN=theta3 EBS3_10=theta4 EBS10=theta5 SURV=exp((1-exp(ESLOPE*(time/24)))/ESLOPE*exp(E0 + ECMIN*CMIN + EBS3_10* BS3_10 + EBS10* BS10)) HAZ=exp(E0 + ESLOPE*(time/24) + ECMIN*CMIN + EBS3_10*BS3_10 + EBS10*BS10) CMIN is minimum/trough TPM concentration (mg/L) BS3_10 is having a baseline number of 3-10 seizures per 3 months (0 is <3 or >10, 1 is between 3-10) BS10 is having a baseline number of at least 10 seizures per 3 months (0 is <10, 1 is >10) SURV is a survival function (chance of not having had at least one seizure) HAZ is the hazard function of having had at least one seizure
raw.parameter_values: [PK] theta1=1.21 theta2=0.479 theta3=0.453 theta4=-0.00306 theta5=1.94 theta6=0.686 theta7=0.635 theta8=4.61 theta9=1.14 theta10=0.105 theta11=0.577 theta12=0.0586 omega1=0.105 omega2=0.577 omega3=0.0586 sigma1=0.0648 sigma2=0.0323 [PD] theta1=-3.130 theta2=-0.051 theta3=-0.112 theta4=1.048 theta5=2.411

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
